{{Quote|I've heard enough, ''[…]'' I have important business waiting for me back at my sector.|Moff Hort|<ref name="Specter" />}}
'''Hort''' was a [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] on the [[Imperial Remnant]]'s [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Moff Council]] who was in charge of a [[Sector/Legends|sector]]<ref name="Specter" /> in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]],<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Specter of the Past]]'' to [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]].</ref> he attended a meeting with the other Moffs and [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] on<ref name="Specter" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Imperial [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]].<ref name="Specter" />

After the supreme commander suggested they should surrender to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], Hort gathered his [[Datacard/Legends|datacards]] and attempted to leave while saying that he had important matters waiting for him at his sector. The council's host, Moff [[Vilim Disra]], told him to sit down, and Hort complied. Shortly after, Hort told fellow council member [[Sander]] to stop describing how the Republic would [[Death/Legends|kill]] them if they gave up.<ref name="Specter" /> Eventually, the council agreed to surrender, and Pellaeon signed a [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty|peace treaty]] with the Republic.<ref name="Vision of the Future">''[[Vision of the Future]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Hort first appeared in the [[1997]] novel ''[[Specter of the Past]]'', the first installment of [[Timothy Zahn]]'s ''[[Star Wars: The Hand of Thrawn Duology]]''.<ref name="Specter" />
